name: Lemon Streusel Squares
recipeIngredient: 1/4 pound unsalted butter or margarine, cold and cut into 8 pieces
2 tablespoons unsalted butter or margarine, melted
1/4 cup light sour cream (or regular sour cream)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Line an 8- or 9-inch square baking pan with heavy-duty foil, extending an inch over the sides.
3. Peel the lemons using a sharp vegetable peeler, removing as little of the bitter white pith as possible.
4. Process the peel, sugar, and nuts until finely chopped. Set aside half for topping.
5. Add butter and flour to the processor and pulse until the mixture holds together.
6. Press the pastry evenly into the bottom of the prepared pan.
7. Bake for 20-24 minutes or until edges are golden and top is lightly colored.
8. In a food processor bowl, blend eggs, egg whites, sugar, melted butter, lemon juice, and flour until combined.
9. Pour filling over the baked crust and return to oven. Bake for an additional 10-12 minutes or until filling is barely set.
10. Crumble reserved lemon-nut mixture evenly over the filling.
11. Return to oven and bake for another 8-10 minutes or until filling is set.
12. Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature.
13. Lift foil, place on a cutting board, trim edges, and cut into 1-1/2-inch squares.
14. Store in the refrigerator for several days or freeze up to 1 month. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
